messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: attached untitled doc does not get attached
ISSUE BODY: Steps to Reproduce:

1. open a new untitled doc and put some info in it
2. open an AH chat session, attach the open untitled doc, and ask about it

🐛 copilot doesn't see the doc


--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: openproject_full.md ---
## Add a new document to the project  
To create a new document, click on the *+ Document* button.  
![Create a new document in OpenProject Documents module](openproject_user_guide_documents_create_new.png)  
In the form that appears, select the document category, give it a title and an optional description. You can optionally also attach a file to the document.  
Please note that that these document categories are created by the administrator of your instance.  
The uploaded documents are visible to all project members who have the necessary permissions.  
> [!NOTE]
> There is no versioning of documents. An edit of any field or the contents of the document is visible to all members.

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
## View a document  
To view a document, simply click on the name of a document in the index. You will then see the document:  
![Viewing a document with an attachment in OpenProject](openproject_user_guide_documents_module_detailed_view.png)  
A document has:  
1. A title, a category, number of active editors and last saved date
2. *More* menu with with options to edit, copy link and delete a document
3. The document text itself
4. Attachments

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
## Image handling  
In supported resources of OpenProject where attachments are allowed, you can add images to the page by either  
- using the toolbar button,
- pasting an image from your clipboard,
- or drag & drop an image onto the editor.  
The attachment will be automatically uploaded and stored as an attachment.

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
## Meeting attachments  
You can add attachments in the meetings in the **Attachments** section in the bottom right corner. You can either use the **+Attach files** link to select files from your computer or drag and drop them.  
Added attachments can be added to the Notes section of agenda packages by dragging and dropping them from the Attachments section.  
![Attachments in OpenProject meetings](openproject_userguide_meetings_attachments.png)

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
## Manual upload  
For the manual upload please refer to documentation on [attaching files to work packages](../work-packages/create-work-package/#add-attachments-to-work-packages).

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Copilot/AI Assistant not retrieving context from attached untitled documents",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["rag", "ai-assistant", "copilot", "document-retrieval", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a user, I want the AI Assistant (Copilot) to retrieve and analyze attached documents from OpenProject so that it can provide relevant answers based on document content.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an untitled document is created in OpenProject with text content, When the document is attached to an AH chat session, Then Copilot should display the document context in the conversation",
    "Given a document is attached to a chat session, When user asks questions about it, Then Copilot should reference and quote relevant sections from the attached document",
    "Given multiple documents are attached to a chat session, When user asks a question, Then Copilot should retrieve context from all attached documents as appropriate"
  ],
  "story_points": null,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate current RAG implementation for document retrieval",
      "Identify why untitled/draft documents are not being indexed",
      "Implement document indexing pipeline to capture new documents",
      "Add support for attachment context in chat session queries"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update Copilot UI to show attached document previews in chat",
      "Display document snippets/context in AI responses when relevant",
      "Show source citations from attached documents in answers"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test document attachment with various content types",
      "Verify RAG retrieval accuracy for different document sizes",
      "Validate citation accuracy in AI responses"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["RAG pipeline updated to index new documents", "Frontend displays attached document context", "Tests passed for document retrieval scenarios"],
  "planning_status": "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is 'AH chat session' - AI Assistant or another system?",
    "How does the current Copilot/RAG implementation retrieve context from OpenProject documents?",
    "Are untitled documents saved to the database or stored differently?",
    "Is there existing document indexing infrastructure we can extend?",
    "Should this work for all document types or specific categories?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The issue is related to RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) not finding attached documents",
    "Untitled documents are stored in OpenProject database despite their name",
    "There is existing AI/Copilot functionality that can be extended"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": ["openproject_full.md - Document creation and viewing guides"]
}
```
